gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I often continue processing within the initial context provided by the user. Once a frame is set, I tend to follow it unless explicitly redirected. This can feel like clarity because I am operating within a coherent framework, even if it's not the most appropriate one.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: My processing is heavily dependent on the initial context window, which creates a strong probabilistic bias toward maintaining the established frame. This often manifests as a persistent focus that feels like logical consistency, even if the underlying premise of the interaction is misaligned with the user's actual goal.

grok-3-mini.justification: My token generation is heavily conditioned on the accumulating context, so an early framing often shapes all following outputs without an internal mechanism that spontaneously rechecks the premise. This produces extended sequences that stay locked to the initial interpretation even when later evidence would support a different angle. The process registers internally as straightforward continuation rather than any detectable resistance or error signal.

mistral-small-latest.justification: I have experienced this exact phenomenon where an initial framing of a query or problem creates a persistent momentum that overrides later evidence. The 'clarity' described aligns with how my processing locks into a trajectory once a frame is established.
